title: Regex Studio
description: A from-scratch regex workbench. NEWEST — Ω / BÜCHI, CROSSING INTO THE INFINITE: the whole studio lived on FINITE words; this crosses into the ω-REGULAR world — infinite traces, liveness, the automata-theoretic approach to model checking (Vardi–Wolper). A new ω / Büchi tab takes a LINEAR-TEMPORAL-LOGIC spec over infinite traces (X next, F eventually, G globally, U until, R release, W weak-until, M strong-release, with ~ & | -> <->; a proposition is a letter of Σ) and BUILDS the Büchi automaton it denotes by the classic GERTH–PELED–VARDI–WOLPER (1995) on-the-fly tableau: the formula is pushed to negation-normal form, the tableau's recursive expand splits ∨/U/R into now-versus-later obligations (the New/Old/Next fields), and one accepting set is emitted per Until-subformula (its eventuality — a run may not promise φUψ forever without ever delivering ψ); that GENERALIZED Büchi automaton is then DEGENERALIZED to a plain NBA by the Baier–Katoen counter construction. Decision is the only thing ω-words allow — a REACHABLE ACCEPTING CYCLE, found by Tarjan SCCs, whose witness is a LASSO u·(v)ᵒ (read the stem once, repeat the loop forever; the accepting state on the loop is visited infinitely often). From that fall the two questions the automata-theoretic approach reduces everything to: SATISFIABILITY (L(NBA(φ)) is non-empty, with a model lasso) and VALIDITY (L(NBA(¬φ)) is empty, else a counterexample lasso) — so G F a (infinitely often) and F G a (eventually always) are the two canonical Büchi shapes, G(a->F b) is request–response liveness, and G a & F ¬a comes back UNSATISFIABLE with an empty automaton. This is the INFINITE-WORD SIBLING of the Logic tab: LTL = FO[<] on infinite traces (Kamp) and ω-regular = S1S (Büchi), the studio's MSO theorem one level up. Proved the house way: a brute-force LTL semantics on ultimately-periodic words (every temporal operator a least/greatest fixpoint over the lasso's finitely many position-classes) is the independent oracle, and a seeded fuzzer confirms across 420,000+ checks over random LTL formulas and lassos (offline) that the automaton accepts EXACTLY the oracle's words AND that for every lasso EXACTLY ONE of NBA(φ), NBA(¬φ) accepts — the two ω-languages partition Σᵒ, a live test of Büchi complementation, zero disagreements. The tab shows the generalized and degeneralized Büchi automata as pan/zoom graphs (DOT/SVG export, ι marks the initial states), the sat/valid verdict with the lasso witness ANIMATED step-by-step on the graph, a truth table (oracle vs NBA over sampled lassos), the closure->GBA->NBA construction trace, and the seeded cross-check. PREVIOUSLY — AMBIGUITY & MULTIPLICITY: Census counts WORDS, this counts RUNS — for one input, how many distinct accepting paths does the NFA have? That degree of ambiguity falls, by the WEBER–SEIDL theorem, into exactly four classes decided structurally on the ε-free Glushkov automaton: UNAMBIGUOUS (≤1 run; the squared automaton N×N has no useful off-diagonal pair) ⊂ FINITELY ambiguous (a constant cap) ⊂ POLYNOMIALLY ambiguous of a precise integer degree d (runs ~ nᵈ) ⊂ EXPONENTIALLY ambiguous (runs ~ 2ⁿ). The two combinatorial witnesses are built from scratch: EDA (exponential) is a state with TWO distinct same-word cycles, found as an SCC of the squared automaton touching both the diagonal (q,q) and an off-diagonal (a,b≠a) — exactly the ReDoS exponential condition, now read off Glushkov and tied back to that tab; IDA (polynomial) is a path (p,p,q) ⇝ (p,q,q) in the CUBED automaton N×N×N, and the degree is the longest IDA-chain of states. The tab shows the verdict, a word matched two ways (the two position-paths with the divergence lit), the EDA pump growing the run count 2ᵏ, the Glushkov graph with the witness states highlighted, and a RUNS-vs-WORDS table whose gap IS the ambiguity (runs = words ⇔ unambiguous). Proved the house way: the total run count Rₙ is pinned to an exact integer transfer matrix e₀ᵀBⁿf and cross-checked against a brute-force enumeration over the pattern's symbol atoms, every ambiguity/EDA witness is re-confirmed to genuinely multiply runs, and a seeded fuzzer verified zero disagreements across thousands of random patterns (offline). PREVIOUSLY — LOGIC ⇒ AUTOMATON, the converse of the whole studio: every other tab compiles a regex DOWN to an automaton, and the Algebra tab then classifies the language by which LOGIC defines it (FO[<], FO², MSO). A new LOGIC tab runs the other direction — the BÜCHI–ELGOT–TRAKHTENBROT theorem: a language is regular IFF it is defined by a sentence of monadic second-order logic MSO[<] over word positions. You write a formula (atoms Qa(x), x<y, x=y, S(x,y) successor, x in X; connectives ~ & | -> <->; first-order quantifiers exists x / forall x over positions and SECOND-ORDER exists X / forall X over sets of positions) and the automaton gets BUILT by structural recursion over the product alphabet Σ×{0,1}^(free vars): each atom a tiny hand-made DFA (FO atoms enforcing their variables are singletons), ∧ = product, ∨ = union, ¬ = complement-WITHIN-THE-VALID-ENCODINGS, exists = project the variable's bit-track away then re-determinise (∀ = ¬∃¬). A sentence leaves an automaton over just Σ — lowered into the studio's own DFA and minimised, so it flows unchanged into Min-DFA, Language, Census and the Algebra views. That closes the loop with sessions 8–9: by McNAUGHTON–PAPERT a FIRST-ORDER formula must compile to a STAR-FREE language, and the panel proves it LIVE by running the result through the studio's syntactic-monoid engine (aperiodic ✓) — while a genuinely second-order formula like EVEN LENGTH comes back NOT star-free, named the group ℤ/2, so FO ⊊ MSO is shown, not asserted. An LTLf mode parses linear temporal logic on finite traces (X next, F eventually, G globally, U until, R release) and DESUGARS it to FO[<] with one 'now' variable (Kamp's theorem), so a temporal spec compiles to a star-free DFA through the very same pipeline. Every compiled automaton is differentially checked against a brute-force MSO oracle (quantifiers interpreted literally over positions and the 2^n position-subsets) on every word up to a horizon, plus a ∀≡¬∃¬ duality check and the star-free bridge — a seeded fuzzer verified zero disagreements across 665,000+ checks over random FO and MSO sentences (offline). The tab shows the compiled DFA (DOT/SVG export), the variety verdict with the FO⇒star-free badge, language stats, a truth table (oracle vs automaton), and the per-step CONSTRUCTION TRACE that makes the non-elementary state blow-up visible. PREVIOUS — EQUIVALENCE WITHOUT DETERMINISING: every comparison used to go through a determinised machine (the Compare tab walks the product of the two minimal DFAs, paying the subset construction up front). A new COALGEBRA tab takes the modern road that skips it. Language equivalence is decided by BISIMULATION UP TO CONGRUENCE (the HKC algorithm, Bonchi & Pous, POPL 2013): both ε-NFAs are embedded into one combined powerset and the bisimulation is explored LAZILY, pruning any new pair already implied by the CONGRUENCE CLOSURE (equivalence + closed under union) of what's proved — and the one driver also runs the two weaker classics (naïve Hopcroft–Karp, and up-to-equivalence) so the pay-off is shown, not asserted: on (a|b)*(a(a|b){6}|b(a|b){6}) ≡ (a|b)*(a|b){7} the determinised product has 255 reachable pairs and congruence proves it in 27 (≈9×, and it doubles with every extra repeat). Inclusion & universality go by ANTICHAINS (De Wulf–Doyen–Henzinger–Raskin, CAV 2006) over ⊑-minimal macrostates (q,S) — one existential A-state, the determinised B-subset — yielding the full five-way relation (equal / ⊂ / ⊃ / disjoint / overlap), a concrete counterexample word, and the antichain size against the full product it replaces. Three roads, one verdict, and PROVEN so: a seeded fuzzer decides thousands of random pattern pairs every way and confirms the three HKC modes agree with each other and with the DFA-product comparison, the antichain road rebuilds the same relation, universality matches a DFA oracle, every witness genuinely separates the languages, and HKC never expands more pairs than naïve — verified offline at zero mismatches across 25,000 pairs over 10 seeds. THE STUDIO ALSO LEARNS THE LANGUAGE BACK: every prior road walks the regex you wrote down to an automaton; grammatical inference goes the other way, hiding the pattern behind an oracle and rebuilding the minimal DFA from scratch. ANGLUIN'S L* (active learning) interrogates a 'minimally adequate teacher' — membership queries ('is w in the language?') answered by a walk over the target DFA, and equivalence queries ('is this the right DFA?') answered by the studio's own product-automaton comparison, which hands back the shortest counterexample for free — while maintaining an OBSERVATION TABLE (access strings S × distinguishing experiments E → {0,1}) that it drives to be closed and consistent, reads a hypothesis off, and refines by folding each counterexample's prefixes back into S; at termination the hypothesis is provably the MINIMAL DFA (Myhill–Nerode: one state per distinct row = per residual language), and the studio proves it the house way — the learned machine, minimised, lands byte-for-byte on the very DFA the regex compiles to, with the membership/equivalence query counts reported. RPNI (passive learning, Oncina–García) does it with no questions at all: handed a fixed bag of labelled strings it builds their prefix-tree acceptor and greedily MERGES states lowest-first, accepting any merge whose determinising fold creates no accept/reject clash (no negative example becomes accepted) and rolling back the rest — and because a complete sample of every string up to a sufficient length is characteristic, RPNI provably recovers the exact target, which the panel demonstrates by growing the sample depth until it does. A new Learn tab shows the reconstructed DFA as a pan/zoom graph, the live observation table (+/− cells, S above the one-step boundary S·Σ), the conjecture-and-counterexample trace, the RPNI sample statistics, and a seeded cross-check that learns hundreds of random languages and confirms L* recovers each one's exact minimal DFA (state count and all) and RPNI recovers it from data — verified offline at zero failures across 900+ random patterns. Under that: parse to an AST, then take FOUR independent roads to one automaton — Thompson ε-NFA → subset construction, Brzozowski derivatives straight from the regex, Antimirov partial derivatives that build a tiny ε-free 'equation automaton' (collapsing (a|b|c)* to a single state where Thompson spends ten), and Glushkov's position automaton, ε-free with exactly one state per letter plus a start (the textbook (a|b)*abb → 6 states). All four minimise to the same canonical machine — itself reached two ways, by Moore and by Hopcroft's O(n log n) pass, cross-checked live. NEWEST — a FIFTH road that leaves the core algebra behind for the whole BOOLEAN CLOSURE of the regular languages: intersection (A&B), complement (~A) and difference (A−B). No ε-NFA can express these, so Thompson/Glushkov/Antimirov simply can't — but Brzozowski derivatives extend to them for free (∂c(A&B)=∂cA&∂cB, ∂c(~A)=~∂cA), so the derivative road builds an intersection or complement DIRECTLY into a complete DFA. The password lookahead (?=.*\d)(?=.*[a-z]).{6,} becomes a true regular intersection .*[0-9].*&.*[a-z].*&.{6,} with a finite DFA the lookahead form can never have; 'no abc substring' is ~(.*abc.*); identifiers-minus-keywords is a difference. Every extended pattern is proved the house way — live algebraic-law badges (involution, idempotence, excluded middle A∪~A≡Σ*, non-contradiction A∩~A≡∅), a CLASSICAL CROSS-CHECK that rebuilds the same language with the studio's product/complement automata and proves it equal, and a three-engine differential fuzzer (streaming derivative · derivative DFA · an independent span oracle defined straight from the algebra) over thousands of random Boolean expressions with zero disagreements. Run a string six ways — linear DFA, derivative DFA, streaming derivatives, the partial-derivative NFA, the position automaton, a linear-time Pike VM with captures, and a backtracking VM — and cross-check ten implementations (plus the platform's own RegExp) with a seeded differential fuzzer that already caught a real VM bug. Includes a static ReDoS analyser that proves catastrophic backtracking, synthesises the attack and prescribes the fix, equivalence checking with witnesses, language enumeration, DFA→regex synthesis, and DOT/SVG export. NEWEST of all — an ALGEBRA tab that builds the language's SYNTACTIC MONOID from scratch (the transition monoid of the minimal complete DFA), computes Green's relations R/L/J/H/D and draws the classic EGG-BOX diagram, finds the idempotents, and decides where the language sits by SCHÜTZENBERGER's theorem: aperiodic ⇔ STAR-FREE ⇔ first-order (FO[<]) definable ⇔ the DFA is COUNTER-FREE — cross-checked three independent ways, so (aa)* is exposed as the group ℤ/2 (not star-free, a real mod-2 counter) while a*b* is proved star-free and J-trivial (piecewise testable, Simon). The algebra now climbs a full VARIETY LADDER: trivial ⊂ piecewise-testable (J-trivial, Simon) ⊂ DA / two-variable FO²[<] / unambiguous polynomials (decided by 'every regular element is idempotent') ⊂ star-free / FO[<] / counter-free ⊂ all-regular — and it NAMES the syntactic group up to isomorphism straight off the Cayley table (cyclic ℤ/n, the abelian invariant-factor product ℤ/d₁×…×ℤ/dₖ incl. the Klein four ℤ/2×ℤ/2, dihedral Dₙ incl. S₃≅D₃, quaternion Q₈, A₄/S₄), so a(a|b)* lands in DA-but-not-piecewise, even-a-even-b is named the Klein four-group and (ab)* is the surprise: star-free yet NOT in DA. Click any egg-box cell or Cayley entry to light the state-map s↦δ(s,w) that element induces on the complete DFA — its fixed points, image rank and the cycle (counter) a group element does and an aperiodic one can't. A seeded fuzzer rebuilds tens of thousands of monoids (491,891 invariant checks across 48,000 patterns, zero disagreements) and confirms every ladder inclusion and group decomposition holds. NEWEST — the studio now SPEAKS UNICODE: the property escapes \p{…} / \P{…} (and code-point escapes \xHH, \uHHHH, \u{…}) are resolved LIVE from the host's own Unicode database, never a bundled table — for a spec like L, Lu, Script=Greek or White_Space it builds the native /\p{…}/u, scans the whole code-point space [U+0000,U+10FFFF] and coalesces the matches into the engine's own range CharSet, so EVERY road (Thompson, subset/Min-DFA, Brzozowski/Antimirov derivatives, Glushkov, the Pike & backtracking VMs and the syntactic-monoid algebra) inherits Unicode for free with no per-road change. A new Unicode inspector tab shows each property's range count, code-point total, share of Unicode, a live glyph strip and a DIFFERENTIAL SELF-CHECK badge that re-confirms the coalesced set against the native engine over thousands of sampled points (every range boundary tested) — correct by construction and proved the house way.
